task: Write a Python function sum_distinct(nums) returning the sum of the distinct values in the list, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution is straightforward, well-written, and correctly handles the task. However, no explicit mention of an edge case in the code is found.
trace: In this solution, we first convert the list to a set which automatically removes any duplicate values. Then we use Python's built-in `sum()` function to add up all the elements in the set. The edge case I handled is when the input list is empty; it returns 0 as expected. This approach ensures the code is concise, readable, and efficient by using sets for removing duplicates.
